frameworks.name: inverted encoding modeling (IEM)
frameworks.one_line: prior method used to recover stimulus-specific neural codes for memory items

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network with LSTM
frameworks.one_line: trained RNN as a mechanistic model of working memory prioritization

frameworks.name: demixed PCA
frameworks.one_line: dimensionality reduction separating priority and stimulus subspaces in hidden activity

core_question: How does the brain represent prioritized versus unprioritized items held in working memory?
key_assumptions: unprioritized items remain actively encoded rather than passively stored
RNN hidden-layer activity can model neural WM representations
PCA-style subspace decomposition reveals priority-specific coding axes
manipulations: functional status of memory items (prioritized vs unprioritized)
comparison across LSTM model activity and human EEG
key_findings: LSTM hidden states show a representational flip between UMI and PMI states
dPCA reveals separate UMI and PMI subspaces with reversed stimulus coding axes
EEG dPCA shows analogous priority-based representational transformations
interpretations: priority-based code reversal protects unprioritized memories from interference
flip-style transformation supports retention while gating behavioral readout
limitations_noted: EEG evidence shows some differences from the model result
